name: Sekoliko
description: Open source school management web application for school and university operations, including fee management, reporting, attendance, and administration workflows.
madagascar_relevance: The project README explicitly describes Sekoliko as a SaaS app for schools and universities in Madagascar, making it directly relevant to local education administration needs.
notes: The repository README and GitHub topics indicate a Symfony-based school management platform. Status is marked 'unknown' because current maintenance level was not confidently verified during manual review.

name: Madagascar Map
description: Open dataset repository listing Malagasy regions, districts, communes, towns, and fokontany, with JSON files intended for reuse in applications and APIs.
madagascar_relevance: The repository provides structured administrative and locality data for Madagascar, including regions, districts, communes, and fokontany, which is directly useful for local mapping, public-service, delivery, and civic applications.
notes: The repository README describes the project as a list of Malagasy administrative divisions and localities, with possible API and geographic extensions. Status is marked 'unknown' because current maintenance level was not confidently verified during manual review.

name: PCG 2005
description: Structured data repository for the PCG 2005 chart of accounts used in Malagasy accounting.
madagascar_relevance: The repository contains structured data for the Madagascar PCG 2005 accounting framework, which is directly relevant for local accounting, bookkeeping, ERP, and financial software.
notes: The repository README describes the project as structured PCG 2005 data for Madagascar. License is marked 'unknown' because a reusable license could not be confidently verified from the repository page reviewed. Status is marked 'unknown' because current maintenance level was not confidently verified during manual review.

name: Gasy Corpus Crawler
description: Python crawler toolkit for scraping Malagasy-language websites and building text corpora for dataset creation.
madagascar_relevance: The repository is specifically designed to scrape Malagasy-language websites and build Malagasy text corpora, making it directly useful for local NLP, language modeling, and corpus creation work.
notes: The README presents the project as a set of scripts for corpus scraping with both BeautifulSoup and Selenium variants. The small commit history and script-oriented design suggest an experimental maturity level.

name: MMCLI
description: Command-line Malagasy encyclopedia and dictionary client that queries Malagasyword.org and returns searchable word data.
madagascar_relevance: The tool is built specifically around Malagasy dictionary and encyclopedia lookup, which makes it directly relevant to Malagasy language access and language-tooling workflows.
notes: The README states that the latest release is the only stable version and that the rest remains a work in progress with unresolved issues, so 'experimental' is the safest catalogue status.

name: Mada CLI
description: CLI for searching Malagasy regions, districts, communes, and fokontany, with support for local geographic lookup and a web UI.
madagascar_relevance: The project is built around Madagascar's administrative and locality structure, including regions, districts, communes, and fokontany, which is directly useful for local search, address handling, and territorial data workflows.
notes: The repository has multiple releases, installation instructions, and a mature usage section, which supports a 'stable' catalogue status.

name: Rakibolana Malagasy
description: Dataset repository containing Malagasy words and their meanings in CSV, JSON, and SQLite formats.
madagascar_relevance: The repository contains Malagasy lexical data and definitions, which is directly useful for dictionary tooling, search, NLP resources, and broader Malagasy language preservation work.
notes: The repository README says the data is free to use and provides CSV, JSON, and SQLite exports, but a formal open source license could not be confidently verified from the repository page reviewed. Status is marked 'unknown' because current maintenance level was not confidently verified during manual review.

name: Pocketenv
description: Open sandbox runtime for agents and humans that provides portable development environments across multiple backend providers.
framework: Multi-backend sandbox CLI and SDK platform
madagascar_relevance: The repository was identified as being built by Malagasy developers, which makes it a relevant developer tool contribution from the local ecosystem and a reusable building block for Madagascar-based software teams.
notes: The README describes Pocketenv as a universal sandbox runtime for agents and humans and explicitly warns that it is still in early development, so 'experimental' is the safest catalogue status.
